stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant release in the LLM space.
It is tracked on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its active use and evaluation within the ML community.
The context points to its application in text-generation tasks using standard transformer frameworks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major industry players in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI's former CTO, Mira Murati, testified that CEO Sam Altman misled her regarding AI model safety standards.
The testimony was provided under oath during the ongoing Musk v. Altman trial.
Murati alleged that Altman falsely claimed OpenAI's legal department approved a new AI model's safety.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet soaring energy demands.
The increased energy consumption is directly linked to the massive global demand for AI chips.
The situation highlights a critical energy crunch facing Taiwan's leading semiconductor manufacturer.

stories.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
stories.author: David Chin
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Design Conductor 2.0,' an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomous hardware design.
The system successfully built a TurboQuant inference accelerator in just 80 hours, demonstrating significant capability scaling.
This work highlights the rapid advancement of LLM agents, which can now handle vastly larger and higher-quality engineering tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating a significant performance upgrade for local AI deployment.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools), highlighting a focus on open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents can now "dream," sort of
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic's Claude is introducing a new capability described as 'dreaming' for its managed agents, suggesting advanced reasoning or planning.
This development points to ongoing advancements in autonomous AI agents and complex task execution.
Usage limits for Claude Code are being increased (doubled) for Pro and Max subscribers.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel method for hallucination detection in LLMs.
Addresses limitations of existing methods like self-consistency, which require costly repeated decoding.
Proposes a computationally efficient alternative by analyzing the normalized entropy of top-K logits at the initial token.

stories.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
stories.author: Mohamed Akrout
bullet_points: Proposes a novel, low-cost method to detect LLM hallucinations by treating the LLM as a black-box dynamical system.
The technique involves projecting LLM responses into a high-dimensional manifold using an embedding model to analyze vector sequences.
This approach offers a computational alternative to existing, expensive sampling-based or knowledge-retrieval methods for hallucination detection.

stories.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling
stories.author: Yan Zhou
bullet_points: Identifies a fundamental, provable trade-off in long-context modeling for sequence models.
The 'Impossibility Triangle' dictates that models cannot simultaneously achieve high Efficiency, Compactness, and Recall.
Formalizes this trade-off using an Online Sequence Processor abstraction, unifying various model types (Transformers, SSMs, etc.).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests tracking the model's performance via standard ML tooling (TensorBoard, safetensors).

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is beginning to shift operational focus to Vandenberg Space Force Base.
Vandenberg is anticipated to become SpaceX's most active launch site in the near term.
The move suggests a strategic evolution or diversification in SpaceX's launch infrastructure.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic has increased usage limits for its Claude Code model.
The company secured a new partnership deal with SpaceX.
This new deal aligns with Anthropic's existing partnerships with major tech players like Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Report: SpaceX IPO gives Musk unchecked power and forbids investor lawsuits
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: SpaceX's potential IPO structure reportedly includes a waiver of investor lawsuit rights.
The report suggests this structure could grant Elon Musk unchecked power over the company.
The news touches on corporate governance and investor protection in high-profile tech firms.

stories.title: Google DeepMind partners with EVE Online for AI model testing
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Google DeepMind is collaborating with EVE Online for AI model testing.
The partnership occurs as CCP Games undergoes a major corporate transition, spending $120M to become independent.
CCP Games is rebranding as Fenris Creations following this significant financial and structural change.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
Outlier tokens, characterized by high norms but limited local information, are found in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.
This work explores how pretrained Vision Transformer (ViT) encoders contribute to this token phenomenon in generative models.

stories.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing rapidly growing working context in long-horizon search agents.
Proposes adaptive context management, maintaining trajectory details based on current task relevance.
Introduces Context-ReAct, a method to operationalize this principle for efficient agent reasoning.

stories.title: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Cloning for On-Robot Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Thomas Weng
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation of Behavior Cloning (BC) in robotics: the lack of self-guided online improvement after initial data collection.
It tackles the issue of distribution mismatch in offline-to-online learning, where new policies can degrade previously learned good actions.
The proposed solution, Q2RL, introduces Q-Estimation and Q-Gating mechanisms to efficiently bridge the gap between offline demonstrations and online Reinforcement Learning.

stories.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation
stories.author: Fei Xia
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of analyzing Whole-Slide Images (WSIs) in histopathology, which are gigapixel-resolution medical images.
It proposes a 'Geometry-Aware State Space Model' as a new paradigm to improve WSI representation, moving beyond standard Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) approaches.
The work aims to enhance the accuracy of disease diagnosis and treatment planning by effectively aggregating information from thousands of image patches.

stories.title: Aes3D: Aesthetic Assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting
stories.author: Weidong Cai
bullet_points: Addresses the need for aesthetic assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), a growing technology in immersive media.
Identifies a gap in current 3D evaluation methods, which focus on fidelity/realism rather than higher-level aesthetics (composition, harmony).
Introduces 'Aes3D' as a method to tackle the challenge of quantifying visual appeal in 3D scenes.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
Outlier tokens, characterized by high norms, are shown to exist in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.
This work explores how tokens from pretrained Vision Transformers (ViTs) behave in generative models, a topic previously underexplored.

stories.title: Estimating the expected output of wide random MLPs more efficiently than sampling
stories.author: Paul Christiano
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method to estimate the expected output of wide Random MLPs without requiring Monte Carlo sampling.
The technique leverages mathematical tools like cumulants and Hermite polynomials to approximate activation distributions layer-wise.
This offers a potentially more efficient alternative to traditional sampling methods for calculating expected loss in ML models.

stories.title: Understanding In-Context Learning for Nonlinear Regression with Transformers: Attention as Featurizer
stories.author: Rongjie Lai
bullet_points: The paper investigates In-Context Learning (ICL) in the context of nonlinear regression using Transformers.
It focuses on providing a theoretical understanding of ICL, moving beyond existing linear model assumptions.
The research proposes using the attention mechanism to explicitly construct transformer networks for this purpose.

stories.title: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The paper investigates how Transformer architectures function when applied to time series forecasting, a less explored area than NLP.
It addresses the performance gap between complex Transformers and simpler models like DLinear by using mechanistic interpretability techniques (Sparse Autoencoders).
The research aims to provide a mechanistic explanation for why simpler models remain competitive in time series forecasting tasks.

stories.title: What Matters in Practical Learned Image Compression
stories.author: Oren Rippel
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap between theoretical and practical learned image compression codecs.
A key advantage of learned codecs is their potential to be optimized specifically for the human visual system (perceptual quality).
The study focuses on jointly optimizing a practical learned codec for both perceptual quality and runtime efficiency.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel, potentially more efficient method for detecting AI hallucinations.
Addresses limitations of existing methods like self-consistency, which require multiple decoding passes and can be sensitive to lexical variation.
Proposes a computationally lighter alternative to semantic self-consistency, which currently incurs high sampling and external inference overhead.

stories.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics
stories.author: Constantine Dovrolis
bullet_points: Proposes a novel approach to continual knowledge updating in LLMs by mimicking biological memory dynamics.
Argues that current explicit external memory systems should evolve toward an associative memory model.
The proposed 'Memini' system aims to allow new associations to be immediately usable and manage memory decay naturally.

stories.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
stories.author: Xiaoli Fern
bullet_points: Introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline for auditing LLM behavior.
The method compares generations from a base model ($M_1$) and an intervention model ($M_2$) under aligned prompts.
It generates human-readable, statistically validated hypotheses detailing behavioral differences caused by interventions.

stories.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
stories.author: Marcin Moskalewicz
bullet_points: Researchers used psychometric questionnaires on 50 LLMs to map out differences between models.
The primary axis of variation found separates models based on their perceived 'phenomenal experience' (e.g., empathy, inner speech) versus simple behavioral reactivity.
This suggests a potential dimension of LLM capability related to subjective or embodied experience, though the variance explained ($R^2_{adj}=.037$) is relatively small.

stories.title: cursed_browser: A web browser with no rendering engine — the VLM reads the HTML and hallucinates the page
stories.author: github.com via aoeu
bullet_points: Introduces 'cursed_browser,' a novel web browser concept.
The browser notably lacks a traditional rendering engine.
It utilizes a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to interpret HTML and 'hallucinate' the page content.

stories.title: AgentCore Harness runs your agent. ShapeV2 controls what it's allowed to do
stories.author: vidanov
bullet_points: AWS has released AgentCore Harness in public preview.
The tool addresses infrastructure challenges related to running AI agents.
ShapeV2 is mentioned as a component controlling the operational scope of the agents.
